name: amd-gpu-operator
description: The AMD GPU Operator manages AMD GPU resources in a Kubernetes cluster and automates tasks related to bootstrapping GPU nodes (drivers, device plugin, NFD, KMM, metrics, DRA, auto-remediation).
overview:
# Overview
The AMD GPU Operator uses the operator framework within Kubernetes to automate the management of AMD software components needed to provision GPUs. For a detailed overview of the operator's architecture and components, see the [GPU Operator Overview](https://instinct.docs.amd.com/projects/gpu-operator/en/latest/overview.html).

## What's New in v1.5.1-beta.0
v1.5.1-beta.0 is a maintenance/beta release that builds on the v1.5.0 feature set (DRA, ANR, NPD integration, enhanced KMM configuration, global image pull secrets, custom package repositories, and metrics exporter enhancements — all described below). It bumps the operator controller, utils, and device metrics exporter images to `v1.5.1-beta.0`t s. The DRA driver (`v1.0.0`), device plugin (`1.31.0.10`), KMM (`v1.0.0`), and NFD (`v0.18.3`) components are unchanged.

## v1.5.0 Feature Set
### Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA) Driver
An alternative to the traditional Device Plugin using the Kubernetes DRA API (`resource.k8s.io/v1`). Deploys `rocm/k8s-gpu-dra-driver` to expose GPUs as structured resources. DRA and the Device Plugin are mutually exclusive — enable only one per `DeviceConfig`. DRA is GA on Kubernetes 1.35+ (NKP v2.18.0+) and CDI is enabled by default in containerd 2.1+, so all prerequisites are met out of the box.

### Auto Node Remediation (ANR)
Automatically remediates unhealthy GPU worker nodes using Argo Workflows. Configurable drain policies, recovery policies, parallel workflow limits, and reboot timeouts. Disabled by default — requires `remediation.enabled: true` in Helm values and Argo Workflows v4.0.3.

### Node Problem Detector (NPD) Integration
Surfaces GPU hardware issues (e.g. inband-RAS errors) as Kubernetes node conditions, which can trigger ANR. Requires separate NPD installation.

### Enhanced KMM Configuration
New `kmm.watch` parameter provides independent control over KMM installation vs. usage. NKP default: `kmm.enabled: false, kmm.watch: true` — uses the shared `amd-kmm-operator` instance.

### Global Image Pull Secrets
`spec.commonConfig.imageRegistrySecrets` and `global.imagePullSecrets` inject pull secrets into all operator-managed workloads, simplifying air-gapped deployments.

### Custom Package Repository URLs
`spec.driver.imageBuild.packageRepoURL` and `gpgKeyURL` allow overriding `repo.radeon.com` for environments where it is mirrored or unreachable.

### Device Metrics Exporter Enhancements
- GPU Agent now communicates via Unix Domain Socket (`/var/run/gpuagent.sock`)
- New metrics: `GPU_PROCESS_CU_OCCUPANCY`, `GPU_ECC_DEFERRED_*`
- Configurable profiler `SamplingInterval` and health `PollingRate`

## Key Features
### Automated GPU Driver Builds
Automatically builds and loads out-of-tree AMD GPU kernel modules using KMM and Kaniko for in-cluster driver compilation. The default `DeviceConfig` targets nodes labeled `feature.node.kubernetes.io/amd-gpu: "true"` (physical GPUs). For SR-IOV virtual function (VF) environments, create an additional `DeviceConfig` with selector `feature.node.kubernetes.io/amd-vgpu: "true"`.

### Device Plugin and Node Labelling
Deploys the AMD device plugin to expose GPU resources to the Kubernetes scheduler and a node labeller for automatic GPU topology discovery.

### Metrics Integration
Includes a built-in metrics exporter that provides Prometheus-format GPU telemetry.

## Configuration
The chart auto-creates a `DeviceConfig` CR named `default` targeting `amd-gpu` labeled nodes. Users configure the driver image registry, TLS, and build settings via Helm value overrides.

For vGPU (SR-IOV VF) nodes, create a second `DeviceConfig` with `spec.selector: { feature.node.kubernetes.io/amd-vgpu: "true" }`.

### Config Overrides (Private Registry)
When enabling this operator with a private registry, supply config overrides that align with the `kmm-registry-credentials` secret created for the AMD KMM Operator. Replace the registry host, port, and project with your values. For the full list of overrides, see [values.yaml](https://github.com/ROCm/gpu-operator/blob/v1.5.1-beta.0/helm-charts-k8s/values.yaml).
```
deviceConfig:
  spec:
    driver:
      enable: true
      blacklist: true
      version: "30.20.1"
      image: "<registry-host>:<port>/<project>/amdgpu_kmod"
      imageBuild:
        baseImageRegistry: "<registry-host>:<port>/<project>"
        baseImageRegistryTLS:
          insecure: false
          insecureSkipTLSVerify: false
      imageRegistrySecret:
        name: "kmm-registry-dockerconfig"
      imageRegistryTLS:
        insecure: false
        # Set to true for registries using custom/self-signed certificates.
        # Kaniko build pods do not inherit the node's CA trust store, and the
        # GPU operator does not propagate this TLS setting to the KMM Module CR.
        # See the workaround in the Troubleshooting section below.
        insecureSkipTLSVerify: false
```
- `blacklist` — when `true`, unloads the in-tree `amdgpu` kernel module before loading the DKMS version. Required on nodes that ship a built-in `amdgpu` driver (most Ubuntu kernels). Without this, the in-tree driver claims the GPU first and the DKMS driver fails to load.
- `version` — the amdgpu DKMS driver version to build. Must match a ROCm release that supports your GPU generation. See the [User and AMD GPU Driver (amdgpu) support matrix](https://rocm.docs.amd.com/projects/install-on-linux/en/latest/reference/user-kernel-space-compat-matrix.html) for the full compatibility table.
- `image` — the registry path where built GPU driver images are pushed/pulled. Do not include a tag; the operator manages tags automatically.
- `imageRegistrySecret.name` — must be `kmm-registry-dockerconfig`, which is auto-created by the AMD KMM Operator reconciler.
- `imageBuild.baseImageRegistry` — the private mirror hosting OS base images (e.g. `ubuntu:24.04`) used during Kaniko builds. This avoids Docker Hub rate limits.
- `insecure`/`insecureSkipTLSVerify` — set to `true` for registries using self-signed or private CA certificates. **Note:** Kaniko build pods do not inherit the node's CA trust store, and the GPU operator currently does not propagate `imageRegistryTLS` to the KMM Module CR's `registryTLS` field. As a workaround, scale down the GPU operator controller, patch the Module CR's `registryTLS.insecureSkipTLSVerify` to `true`, and scale back up after the build completes.

### DRA Driver Configuration (Optional)
To use the DRA driver instead of the traditional Device Plugin:
```
deviceConfig:
  spec:
    draDriver:
      enable: true
      image: "docker.io/rocm/k8s-gpu-dra-driver:v1.0.0"
    devicePlugin:
      enableDevicePlugin: false
```
All prerequisites (DRA GA, CDI) are met on NKP v2.18.0+ (K8s 1.35+, containerd 2.1+).

### Global Image Pull Secrets (Optional)
Instead of configuring `imageRegistrySecret` on each component, set a global pull secret:
```
global:
  imagePullSecrets:
    - name: "kmm-registry-dockerconfig"
```
Or at the DeviceConfig level:
```
deviceConfig:
  spec:
    commonConfig:
      imageRegistrySecrets:
        - name: "kmm-registry-dockerconfig"
```

### Custom Package Repository (Optional)
For air-gapped environments mirroring `repo.radeon.com`:
```
deviceConfig:
  spec:
    driver:
      imageBuild:
        packageRepoURL: "https://my-mirror.internal/amdgpu/ubuntu/30.20.1"
        gpgKeyURL: "https://my-mirror.internal/rocm.gpg.key"
```

## Troubleshooting: In-Tree Module Conflict
The `driver.blacklist: true` setting prevents the in-tree `amdgpu` kernel module from loading on reboot. However, if the module was already loaded before the operator was installed, the blacklist has no effect until the next reboot. A node reboot is required for the blacklist to take effect in this case.

## Dependencies
- **AMD KMM Operator** — provides the shared KMM instance. Strongly recommended to enable first; without it, driver builds will not function. To use the chart's embedded KMM subchart instead of the shared catalog app, set `kmm.enabled: true` and `kmm.watch: false` in your config overrides and skip enabling `amd-kmm-operator`. You must also manually create the `kmm-registry-dockerconfig` secret (normally auto-created by the KMM Operator reconciler) with your registry credentials — see [Registry Secret Configuration](https://instinct.docs.amd.com/projects/gpu-operator/en/latest/drivers/installation.html#registry-secret-configuration).
- **Node Feature Discovery** — provided by the NKP platform layer (disabled in this chart).
- **Argo Workflows v4.0.3** — required only if enabling Auto Node Remediation (`remediation.enabled: true`).

- [DRA Driver Documentation](https://instinct.docs.amd.com/projects/gpu-operator/en/latest/dra/dra-driver.html)
- [Auto Node Remediation](https://instinct.docs.amd.com/projects/gpu-operator/en/latest/autoremediation/auto-remediation.html)
- [Node Problem Detector](https://instinct.docs.amd.com/projects/gpu-operator/en/latest/npd/node-problem-detector.html)
